title: junellia crithmifolia
intro: Junellia crithmifolia, also known as Chilca, is a shrub native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. Traditionally, Junellia crithmifolia has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, and its leaves have been used to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Junellia crithmifolia can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and made into a powder for use in herbal remedies.
